commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8ee8f7b2fdef46fb541e39cc62828ea59e99ede9)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds and adjusts Telegram target-writeback regression coverage around gateway scope handling and documents the existing helper authorization rule.

PR surface: Source +37, Tests +25. Total +62 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence failing current-main reproduction is needed here: current main already contains the admin-scope writeback guard, and this PR is now mainly tests plus a comment. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer handling is needed because the PR is protected and authorization-sensitive, and the remaining proof gap cannot be supplied by an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(telegram): require admin for target writeback [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Requires explicit `operator.admin` gateway scope before Telegram target resolution writes back to user config or cron delivery targets.
- Preserves non-gateway/internal Telegram sends by treating absent scope metadata as the existing internal writeback path.
- Avoids adding a new writeback authority field or changing shared channel-action dispatch semantics.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes none.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related maintainer-requested hardening for Telegram target writeback authorization.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Gateway-originated Telegram target writeback now requires `operator.admin`, while internal/non-gateway sends without scope metadata still can persist resolved `t.me`/`@channel` targets.
- Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree focused tests plus delegated Blacksmith Testbox changed gate.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.ts extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.test-shared.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.ts extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.test-shared.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`.
- Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 5 files / 260 tests. Local oxlint, oxfmt, and diff-check passed. Autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported. Testbox `tbx_01kt12s3jg15esy1skp891cp87` used provider `blacksmith-testbox`, lanes `extensions, extensionTests`, exit 0.
- Observed result after fix: Explicit non-admin gateway scope arrays skip config/cron writeback; `operator.admin` gateway scopes allow writeback; absent scope metadata still represents the internal/non-gateway path through `sendMessageTelegram`.
- What was not tested: Live Telegram API behavior was not exercised.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The behavioral proof uses mocked Telegram/config/cron boundaries; no real bot token or live chat was used.
- Before evidence: The first helper-level fail-closed shape treated absent scope metadata as unauthorized and would have regressed internal cron/config target writeback. The final patch keeps the helper fail-closed only for explicit gateway scope arrays.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.ts extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.test-shared.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.ts extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.test-shared.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added target-writeback coverage for explicit non-admin/empty gateway scopes and explicit `operator.admin` writeback. Added send-level coverage proving internal target writeback still propagates absent gateway scopes.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The initial helper-level authorization check skipped internal/non-gateway target writeback when scopes were omitted. Autoreview caught that regression; the final patch preserves the internal path.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No schema or migration change. Runtime writeback authorization changes for Telegram target resolution.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Distinguishing gateway callers without admin scope from internal/non-gateway callers that legitimately omit scope metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

The fail-closed check only rejects explicit gateway scope arrays lacking `operator.admin`; lower-level/internal sends still leave scopes absent, and gateway server paths already supply client scopes or an empty array.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for GitHub Actions and ClawSweeper/Real behavior proof checks on head `8ee8f7b2fdef46fb541e39cc62828ea59e99ede9`.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub Actions are pending for the latest pushed head.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed autoreview findings about internal cron/config target writeback regression and wrong-layer scope defaulting.
